theme: The Real ROI of Incentive Travel — 2026 Numbers

linkedin.text:
"It's a nice-to-have." That's how most CFOs describe incentive travel. The data says otherwise.

We analyzed 12 programs we ran in 2025. The ROI isn't a feeling — it's a number:

📊 6 months post-trip:
• Sales teams: +18-31% above baseline
• Operations: +14% efficiency
• NPS: +22 points vs. non-trip years

👥 Retention:
• 87% of participants stayed 12+ months (vs. 61% company-wide)
• One retained senior hire = €50-150k saved

💡 Engagement:
• 2.4x higher engagement scores
• Managers spend 35% less time on conflict resolution

📈 The bottom line:
• €3,500/person spent → €12,000-18,000/person in retention value
• ROI: 3.4:1 to 5.1:1

The business case isn't competitive. It's compelling.

💬 Comment "ROI" and we'll send the one-page framework we use to model it.

#EventROI #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #EmployeeEngagement #B2BEvents #MICE #Leadership

facebook.text:
A finance director once told us a trip was "money we'll never see again." So we showed him the numbers.

We'd just analyzed 12 of our 2025 programs. Here's what they actually returned:

Sales teams performed 18-31% above baseline six months later. 87% of participants were still with the company a year on — versus 61% of similar roles who didn't travel. Spend €3,500 a head, save €12,000-18,000 in retention.

That's a 3.4:1 to 5.1:1 return. Not a feeling. A number.

How does your company measure the value of a team trip? Tell us in the comments. 💬
